axis: println stdout suppressed in remote routing

evidence.p4_plan_md_record: anima-speak/p4_plan.md:33 — 'Runtime stdout suppressed (GATE wrapper quirk, not code error; verified via parse/build).'
evidence.session_observation_2026_04_26: running physical_limits.hexa via hetzner remote produced no visible stdout; exit code 0; same script worked silently for all subsequent invocations
evidence.workaround_used: hexa-only analytic synthesis (raw#9) replaced empirical run; later attempt to write JSON via exec heredoc to file was the only viable evidence channel

root_cause_hypothesis.h1_remote_ssh_pty: hexa_remote may use ssh without -t; stdout buffering on remote side; hexa.real's println output not flushed before connection close
root_cause_hypothesis.h2_resolver_2gt1: hexa-resolver echoes stderr but may swallow stdout when chained with `exec` and explicit redirect
root_cause_hypothesis.h3_docker_exec_TTY: docker exec without -t allocates no pseudo-TTY; stdout is fully buffered, may not flush on quick scripts

concrete_failures.expected: 327-line ASCII report with 7/7 PASS + 14/14 EXACT table

concrete_failures.actual: 0 chars; eventually rc=64 cwd_unmappable → docker fallback also silent

remediation_options.risk: low — pty allocation may break batch pipelines, gate behind env

remediation_options.cost: low — single line in interpreter init

remediation_options.approach: stdout teeing — resolver dumps stdout to /tmp/hexa-stdout.<pid>.log
remediation_options.risk: medium — may break consumers expecting raw stdout

remediation_options.approach: println-to-file convention — accept current limitation, all hexa scripts write evidence to state/*.json via exec heredoc
remediation_options.tradeoff: interactive debugging stays broken; only batch evidence works

verdict: DEBUGGING_VISIBILITY_BUG severity=MEDIUM; observable, well-known (p4_plan documented), workaround exists but ergonomic cost is high
